title: phegopteris fragilis
intro: Phegopteris fragilis, also known as maidenhair fern, is a species of delicate, lacy fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phegopteris fragilis are said to include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried or made into a tea, and can also be used in infusions and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
